Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: Stacked Game of Life 148 upvotes, 24 comments
  2. MyFirst Kids Watch Hacked. Access to Camera and Microphone 67 upvotes, 10 comments
  3. Elevator Saga: The elevator programming game (2015) 81 upvotes, 14 comments
  4. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 35 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 18 upvotes, 1 comments
  6. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide – Unsloth Documentation 155 upvotes, 43 comments
  7. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 127 upvotes, 38 comments
  8. Motorola GrapheneOS devices will be bootloader unlockable/relockable 1138 upvotes, 461 comments
  9. RE#: how we built the fastest regex engine in F# 162 upvotes, 56 comments
  10. “It turns out” (2010) 162 upvotes, 62 comments
  11. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 250 upvotes, 104 comments
  12. Medical journal says the case reports it has published for 25 years are fiction 119 upvotes, 45 comments
  13. Government grant-funded research should not be published in for-profit journals 244 upvotes, 120 comments
  14. A CPU that runs entirely on GPU 210 upvotes, 103 comments
  15. RFC 9849. TLS Encrypted Client Hello 227 upvotes, 113 comments
  16. The 1,700-year-old megastructure history almost forgot 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  17. Sea level much higher than assumed in most coastal hazard assessments 57 upvotes, 23 comments
  18. Agentic Engineering Patterns 402 upvotes, 224 comments
  19. Nobody Gets Promoted for Simplicity 623 upvotes, 369 comments
  20. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 18 upvotes, 5 comments
  21. Making Firefox's right-click not suck with about:config 58 upvotes, 28 comments
  22. Glaze by Raycast 142 upvotes, 83 comments
  23. Emails to Outlook.com rejected due to a fault or overzealous blocking rules 88 upvotes, 56 comments
  24. Factory Logic 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. Greg Knauss Is Losing Himself 54 upvotes, 36 comments
  26. TikTok will not introduce end-to-end encryption, saying it makes users less safe 343 upvotes, 335 comments
  27. Who Writes the Bugs? A Deeper Look at 125,000 Kernel Vulnerabilities 7 upvotes, 2 comments
  28. MacBook Neo 934 upvotes, 1232 comments
  29. New York could prohibit chatbot medical, legal, engineering advice 14 upvotes, 9 comments
  30. Roboflow (YC S20) Is Hiring a Security Engineer for AI Infra 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 3:00am, 5 March 2026. Source here.
